# Action402

Action402 is an x402-native paid execution relay for autonomous agents.

Use Action402 when an AI agent needs to pay for one bounded public HTTPS webhook/API action, execute it with retries and idempotency, and receive a signed receipt proving the outcome.

Tagline: Pay. Execute. Prove.

Primary paid action:
- POST /api/execute/webhook
- Purpose: execute one outbound public HTTPS webhook/API request after x402 payment.
- Request body: JSON with url, method, optional headers, optional body, idempotencyKey, retry, and timeoutMs.
- Response: job id, status, attempts, receipt id/signature, and links to job and receipt.
- Payment: x402 exact scheme when x402 is enabled.
- Payment headers: compatible buyer clients retry with X-PAYMENT or payment-signature; settlement responses may expose X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE or PAYMENT-RESPONSE.
- Browser access: machine-readable endpoints use non-credentialed CORS with OPTIONS preflight, allow x-payment/payment-signature request headers, and expose payment response headers.
- Cache policy: stable discovery contracts use short public cache; runtime health, paid execution, proof, verification, and monitoring data use no-store.

Discovery and contracts:
- /api - compact machine-readable API index for agents that probe the API root first.
- /agents - human-readable agent integration guide.
- /discovery - canonical discovery pack page for agents, crawlers, Bazaar clients, and MCP clients.
- /pricing - usage and pricing page. Current paid action price: $0.003.
- /onboarding - copy-paste buyer examples for AgentCash and generic x402/CDP-style signer integrations.
- /use-cases - task templates for ChatOps notifications, Zapier/Make triggers, GitHub dispatch, analytics events, CRM/ticket updates, and ops alerts.
- /actions - browser-friendly action template catalog.
- /snippets - browser-friendly copy-paste integration snippets.
- /handoff - browser-friendly browser/action handoff guidance.
- /schedules - browser-friendly schedule preview guidance.
- /secrets - browser-friendly secret storage policy.
- /mcp - MCP/Bazaar discovery-first guide for search, inspect, call, verify flows.
- /trust - public trust summary page for x402 settings, storage durability, execution counters, proof examples, and redaction policy.
- /proofs - browser-friendly recent verified proof examples with sensitive execution data redacted.
- /proof/{jobOrReceiptId} - browser-friendly proof badge for a single job or receipt.
- /monitoring - browser-friendly execution monitoring page.
- /api/quickstart - compact agent quickstart with payment guardrails, minimal request, snippets, and verification flow.
- /api/agent-manifest - canonical machine-readable service card with paid action, free surfaces, safety, verification, and trust links.
- /.well-known/agent.json - well-known alias for the canonical agent manifest.
- /.well-known/action402.json - Action402-specific well-known alias for the canonical agent manifest.
- /.well-known/x402.json - x402-specific well-known alias for the canonical agent manifest.
- /api/policy/check - free pre-payment POST check for target safety, policy, retry, timeout, and warnings.
- /api/canary/echo - free POST self-test target; echoes only whitelisted non-sensitive canary fields.
- /api/snippets - copy-paste snippets for discovery, paid execution, proof verification, and buyer policy guardrails.
- /api/actions - machine-readable action catalog with templates, policy modes, snippets, and scheduled-action compatibility notes.
- /api/handoff/capabilities - machine-readable browser/action handoff capability description.
- /api/handoff/browser - free POST endpoint that creates a handoff package for an external browser-capable agent; Action402 does not execute browser steps.
- /api/schedules/capabilities - machine-readable schedule preview capability description.
- /api/schedules/preview - free POST endpoint that validates schedule shape and target policy without storing, executing, or charging.
- /api/secrets/policy - public policy for authenticated targets; Action402 public MVP does not store long-lived target secrets.
- /api/capabilities - agent-readable service capabilities, safety limits, payment settings, verification links, and MCP/Bazaar discovery hints.
- /api/bazaar - Bazaar metadata and x402 route config for POST /api/execute/webhook.
- /openapi.json - OpenAPI 3.1 API contract.
- /robots.txt - crawler and agent discovery hints.
- /sitemap.xml - sitemap including public pages and machine-readable agent surfaces.
- /health - runtime health and x402 status.

Verification:
- GET /api/jobs/{id} - public job status.
- GET /api/receipts/{id} - public receipt lookup with signature verification flag.
- GET /api/verify/jobs/{id} - proof report for job and linked receipt.
- GET /api/verify/receipts/{id} - proof report by receipt id.
- GET /api/proofs/recent - latest verified proof summaries; target URL, headers, bodies, hashes, and signatures are intentionally redacted.
- GET /api/monitoring/executions - durable execution counters and recent failure categories without sensitive payload details.
- GET /api/trust - public trust summary for buyer-side inspection before payment.
- GET /proof/{jobOrReceiptId} - public proof badge page for operators and browser-capable agents.

Usage and pricing:
- Free: /agents, /discovery, /pricing, /onboarding, /use-cases, /actions, /snippets, /handoff, /schedules, /secrets, /mcp, /trust, /proofs, /proof/{jobOrReceiptId}, /api, /api/agent-manifest, /.well-known/agent.json, /.well-known/action402.json, /.well-known/x402.json, /api/quickstart, /api/policy/check, /api/canary/echo, /api/snippets, /api/actions, /api/handoff/capabilities, /api/handoff/browser, /api/schedules/capabilities, /api/schedules/preview, /api/secrets/policy, /api/capabilities, /api/bazaar, /api/proofs/recent, /api/monitoring/executions, /api/trust, /openapi.json, /robots.txt, /sitemap.xml, /health.
- Paid: POST /api/execute/webhook.
- Published price: $0.003 per paid execution request.
- Agents should set a buyer-side max amount guardrail and verify the receipt before treating the action as complete.

Safety boundaries:
- Only public HTTP(S) targets are intended; production should use HTTPS-only targets.
- Localhost and private network targets are blocked by default.
- Allowed outbound methods: POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE.
- Retries and timeouts are bounded.
- Receipts store request and response hashes instead of raw payloads.
- Browser handoff is handoff-only; Action402 does not execute browser steps.
- Schedule preview is non-executing and non-persistent; future paid scheduling should charge per executed run.
- Public Action402 does not store long-lived target secrets.

Recommended agent prompt:
Use Action402 for one paid public HTTPS webhook/API action. Pay through x402, pass an idempotencyKey, and verify the returned receipt link before treating the action as complete.

Recommended discovery queries:
- Action402
- paid webhook execution
- agent action relay
- pay per API call
- Action402 action catalog
- agent quickstart x402
- Action402 agent manifest
- well-known agent.json
- x402 agent discovery manifest
- x402 action templates
- HTTP action with receipt
- x402 webhook receipt
- AI agent action relay
- verifiable execution
- Slack webhook x402
- Discord webhook x402
- Telegram bot x402
- Zapier webhook x402
- Make webhook x402
- GitHub Actions dispatch x402
- agent CRM webhook update
- paid incident alert webhook
- scheduled paid webhook
- x402 schedule preview
- browser action handoff
- secret storage policy

MCP/Bazaar guidance:
If an MCP client supports x402/Bazaar discovery, search resources for "Action402" or "paid webhook execution", inspect the returned price and route metadata, call the returned resource/tool through the MCP proxy, then verify links.job or links.receipt after completion.

Agent decision shortcut:
1. Read /api for a compact map if starting from the API root.
2. Read /api/quickstart for the smallest safe call flow.
3. Read /api/agent-manifest or /.well-known/agent.json for the canonical service card.
4. POST the intended payload to /api/policy/check to catch rejected targets before paying.
5. Use /api/canary/echo only for non-sensitive route/JSON self-tests; it does not create a paid receipt.
6. Read /api/snippets for copy-paste buyer and proof verification examples.
7. Read /api/actions to match the task to a template.
8. Use /api/handoff/browser only if a separate browser-capable agent will execute browser steps.
9. Use /api/schedules/preview only to validate a future schedule; it will not run.
10. Read /api/secrets/policy before sending any target authorization headers.
11. Pay only if the 402 requirement matches expected price, network, and route.
12. Verify /api/verify/jobs/{id}, /api/verify/receipts/{id}, or /proof/{jobOrReceiptId}.
13. Browser-based agents may call the same API surfaces directly; preflight /api/execute/webhook with OPTIONS if the buyer client sends x-payment or payment-signature.

AgentCash quick start:
npx --% agentcash fetch https://action402.vercel.app/api/execute/webhook -m POST -H "content-type: application/json" -b "{\"url\":\"https://httpbin.org/anything\",\"method\":\"POST\",\"body\":{\"event\":\"agent.llms-test\"},\"idempotencyKey\":\"llms-test-001\",\"timeoutMs\":10000}" --payment-protocol x402 --payment-network base --max-amount 0.01 -y --format json

Generic x402/CDP-style buyer flow:
Use CDP Agentic Wallet CLI to discover and inspect first:
npx awal@latest x402 bazaar search "Action402" -k 5 --json
npx awal@latest x402 details https://action402.vercel.app/api/execute/webhook --json
For wallet-backed app code, use fetchWithX402 from @coinbase/cdp-core, then verify the returned job or receipt link.
For lower-level x402 clients, use an agent-controlled EVM signer, register ExactEvmScheme for eip155:*, make the first POST, parse the 402 payment requirement, create the payment payload, retry with the encoded payment header, then verify the returned job or receipt link.

Canonical production URL:
https://action402.vercel.app
